name: env-continuity-bloat
description: Measures environmental fact bloat across repeated walking turns in the same area. Starts player on a named street, walks east for 10 turns, then measures: (1) env fact count in the location attribute bucket, (2) env fact count after injection-time dedup (survivor count from the assembled continuity line), (3) collapse ratio. The dedup pass at assembleContinuityPacket is expected to keep env facts stable at ~5-8 unique canonical facts rather than growing to 20+.

request_template.action: I am standing on Main Street in a quiet town, in front of a row of weathered buildings with cracked pavement and a red-and-white awning. A rusted mailbox stands crooked nearby.
request_template.WORLD_PROMPT: A quiet town on a warm afternoon. Main Street has cracked pavement, faded facades with peeling paint, a red-and-white awning over a shop door, and a rusted mailbox with its flag at half-mast. Weeds push through the concrete seams.
